# KarirKalyan

> Full-stack job application tracker — Rails 8 API + Next.js 16 frontend

KarirKalyan is a web application for tracking job applications through a finite state machine (FSM)
pipeline. Built as a portfolio project by Chairul Akmal.

## What it does

- Tracks job applications across 13 FSM states: wishlist → draft → applied → phone_screen →
  technical → final_round → offer → accepted/declined/rejected/ghosted/withdrawn/archived
- Writes an immutable TimelineEntry on every status transition (full audit trail)
- Sends follow-up reminders via idempotent Sidekiq background jobs
- Stores resume and cover letter as PostgreSQL bytea per application
- Exposes a stats dashboard via pure SQL aggregation (no N+1)
- Cursor-based pagination on the applications list

## Tech stack

- Backend: Rails 8 API-only, Ruby 3.4.9, PostgreSQL 16, Devise + devise-jwt, Sidekiq + Redis 8
- Frontend: Next.js 16 App Router, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS
- Auth: JWT issued by Rails, stored in httpOnly cookie by Next.js — never reaches client JS
- Deploy: Railway (API service + web service + managed PostgreSQL + managed Redis)
- Tests: RSpec (unit + request specs against real DB), Playwright E2E

## Live

- App: https://kk.chairulakmal.com
- API docs (Swagger UI): https://api-production-4899.up.railway.app/api-docs
- Demo account: demo@karirkalyan.com / oretachinomachida (12 pre-loaded Tokyo tech applications)

## Source

https://github.com/chairulakmal/karirkalyan
